title: centaurea balbisiana subsp. aemilii
intro: Centaurea balbisiana subsp. aemilii, also known as Knapweed or Centaurea aemilii, is a perennial plant species within the Asteraceae family. It is native to the Mediterranean region and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as displaying antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, fever, and digestive issues.
